title: Starbucks pilots TikTok program for boosting employee-generated content
tldr: Starbucks is partnering with TikTok to build a custom creator network that turns baristas into brand influencers via employee-generated content. A rare, concrete EGC case study from a major brand.
why_fresh: new EGC program from a flagship brand
angle: What Starbucks' barista-influencer pilot reveals about scaling employee-generated content without losing authenticity.

title: The new news reality: Bigger reach, lower trust
tldr: The Reuters Institute 2026 Digital News Report finds only 37% globally trust news, the lowest ever, while social became the top daily news source. Essential context on shifting media-consumption habits.
angle: How comms teams should respond when reach is up but trust is collapsing.

title: Fewer than 1 in 15 pieces of creator content delivers both audience engagement and ROI
tldr: Kantar data shows the vast majority of creator content fails to deliver both engagement and return, puncturing creator-economy hype with hard numbers.
angle: Why most creator content fails on ROI — and what the winning 1-in-15 do differently.

title: Overwhelmed by news on social? SaySo is betting a smaller, vetted creator feed is the answer
tldr: A new app bets that a curated, trusted creator feed beats the firehose of slop and misinformation as more people get news from creators. A direct response to media-consumption overload.
why_fresh: new product responding to a consumption trend
angle: The pendulum from infinite feeds back to curated trust — what it means for content distribution.

title: Something Novel: For teens, a print renaissance might be afoot
tldr: CJR explores a possible print revival among teens amid social-media fatigue and magazine layoffs. A counterintuitive media-consumption shift worth tracking.
angle: Why Gen Z teens are reaching for print — physicality as the new premium in a slop-filled feed.

title: Why a stylist and her crew built a magazine instead of just moaning about AI
tldr: Anna May and the MISC team made a physical, human magazine in direct response to AI's takeover of editorial imagery. A craft-driven, human-over-polish case study.
angle: Building something physical and human as a creative statement against AI slop.

title: Gen Z is turning Instagram into the new LinkedIn
tldr: A Zety survey finds Gen Z now turns to YouTube and Instagram over LinkedIn for career advice, signalling a major shift in where professional content lives.
angle: What it means for B2B and thought leadership when professional content migrates off LinkedIn.

title: The State of Internal Communications in 2026
tldr: PR Daily argues internal comms has reached a turning point, reframing its strategic role inside organizations. Relevant to Igor's internal comms and employee engagement interests.
why_fresh: annual state-of report with new framing
angle: How internal comms becomes a strategic engine rather than a newsletter factory.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Contently shows top-10 ranked pages may be ignored by Google's AI Overviews, breaking the old SEO playbook. Critical for content strategists rethinking visibility.
why_fresh: new dynamic in AI search visibility
angle: Why ranking #1 no longer guarantees AI visibility — and how to architect for citation.

title: The agency monopoly on creativity has fallen, says Klarna CMO
tldr: Klarna's CMO argues AI and in-housing have ended agencies' grip on creativity, a provocation from Cannes about who makes brand content now.
angle: If the agency creativity monopoly is over, who owns the brand narrative next?

title: As marketers grapple with AI, Cannes leaders say human creativity and authenticity matter more than ever
tldr: Cannes business leaders position human creativity and authenticity as the differentiator amid AI saturation. A recurring human-over-polish theme worth synthesizing.
angle: Is 'human authenticity' the real strategy or just a Cannes talking point this year?

title: The 4 PR and Marketing Metrics that Matter for AI Visibility
tldr: Gini Dietrich identifies the four metrics for AI visibility in 2026 and argues impressions, reach and AVEs won't survive budget scrutiny. Practical measurement update.
why_fresh: new measurement framework for AI era
angle: Retiring vanity metrics: building a measurement model for the AI-search era.

title: Виртуальный СЕО: зачем Цукербергу ИИ-двойник и что не так с этой идеей
tldr: SETTERS examines Zuckerberg's AI-clone CEO concept and whether it repeats the metaverse flop, plus how leaders can actually use AI. A thoughtful executive-positioning angle.
why_fresh: new take on AI executive avatars
angle: AI executive doubles: gimmick or the future of leadership presence and thought leadership?

title: Bottle Rocket Media Celebrates 15 Years of Human-Centered Storytelling with Handcrafted Stop-Motion Campaign
tldr: An agency marks its anniversary with a deliberately handcrafted stop-motion campaign as a statement on human-centered storytelling. A small but on-theme craft case study.
angle: Choosing handmade over AI as a brand-positioning move in 2026.
